collections.name: AI/HPC
collections.summary: Skills for launching, validating, monitoring, demoing, and troubleshooting distributed AI workloads, CPU thread pools, file descriptor pressure, GPU binding, and accelerator visibility on Slurm-backed HPC systems.

collections.name: Bioinformatics Workflows
collections.summary: Domain skills for running reviewed nf-core, GATK, and BLAST bioinformatics workflows on Slurm-backed HPC systems.

collections.name: Containers
collections.summary: Skills for building, validating, running MPI containers, and staging data for containerized HPC workloads.

collections.name: Core HPC
collections.summary: Starter skills for Slurm jobs, arrays, array retry planning, dependency chains, pending reason and maintenance triage, QOS/account limit evidence, OOM, time-limit, node-failure, and output-log triage, efficiency review, file descriptor triage, license-aware jobs, restartable and requeue-safe workflows, notebooks, RStudio, IDE tunnels, OpenMP placement, debugging, node-local scratch staging, shared project permissions, and storage triage.

collections.name: Data Movement
collections.summary: Skills for staging, transferring, validating, profiling, layout planning, benchmarking, permissions and file descriptor triage, and managing research data across HPC filesystems, node-local scratch, and object storage.

collections.name: Facility Operations
collections.summary: Read-only operational skills for usage reporting, pending reason and maintenance triage, QOS/account limit evidence, OOM memory, time-limit, and node-failure triage, file descriptor pressure, node triage, shared project permissions, and module tree health.

collections.name: GPU, MPI, And Performance
collections.summary: Skills for validating GPU allocations, GPU binding, GPU memory failures, TensorBoard training monitors, Ray, JAX, Hugging Face Accelerate, TensorFlow multi-worker training, NCCL communication, DeepSpeed and PyTorch DDP launches, MPI fabric evidence, MPI rank binding, hybrid MPI/OpenMP layouts, BLAS/OpenMP thread pools, CMake build preflight, parallel HDF5/NetCDF preflight, Darshan I/O profile analysis, Lustre striping layout planning, containerized MPI, and mpi4py launches, OpenMP placement, Slurm efficiency review, storage smoke benchmarks, and first-pass performance evidence.

collections.name: Scheduler Basics
collections.summary: Starter skills for submitting and comparing basic jobs across Slurm, PBS-style, LSF, HTCondor, and Grid Engine schedulers, including array retry, common failure, output-log triage, memory triage, time-limit triage, and node-failure triage.

collections.name: Simulation Workflows
collections.summary: Domain skills for scientific simulation planning, MPI/GPU-heavy simulation, MPI fabric evidence, hybrid MPI/OpenMP layouts, rank binding diagnostics, CMake build preflight, parallel HDF5/NetCDF preflight, Darshan I/O profile analysis, Lustre striping layout planning, electronic-structure, CFD, weather, restart, profiling, and storage-smoke workflows on HPC systems.

collections.name: Software Stacks
collections.summary: Skills for debugging modules, checking module tree health, compiler/MPI compatibility, CMake build preflight, parallel HDF5/NetCDF preflight, BLAS/OpenMP thread pools, licensed software jobs, Python, TensorBoard, Streamlit, Open OnDemand templates, Ray, Dask, Parsl, JAX, Hugging Face Accelerate, TensorFlow, mpi4py, R, RStudio, Julia, MATLAB, Conda environments, IDE tunnels, containers, containerized MPI, and reproducible HPC software stacks.

collections.name: Training And Onboarding
collections.summary: Skills for teaching new HPC users, including Slurm jobs, maintenance and reservation triage, array retry planning, output-log triage, OOM memory, time-limit, and node-failure triage, file descriptor limits, BLAS/OpenMP thread pools, node-local scratch staging, shared project permissions, requeue-safe restart behavior, license-aware software use, notebooks, Open OnDemand templates, TensorBoard monitors, Streamlit apps, RStudio, IDE tunnels, Python, R, Julia, and MATLAB workloads, and workshop environments.

collections.name: Workflow Engines
collections.summary: Skills for launching portable workflow engines, CWL/WDL runs, Dask and Parsl worker pools, file descriptor and time-limit triage, lightweight Slurm dependency chains, and safe array retry planning.

site_adapters.name: Example Campus Cluster
site_adapters.summary: A non-production adapter showing how a site can map generic skills to local HPC policy.

site_adapters.summary: A public-doc-backed draft adapter for mapping portable skills to NERSC Perlmutter conventions.

skills.description: This skill helps HPC users and support teams diagnose permission denied failures in shared project, scratch, and collaboration directories without changing ownership, modes, ACLs, or group membership.
skills.name: Shared Project Permissions Triage
skills.summary: Collect read-only evidence for shared project directory permission failures.

skills.description: This skill uses read-only Slurm accounting or an exported sacct table to identify failed array tasks, produce a compact retry array range, and generate a reviewable sbatch retry plan before resubmission.

examples.title: Mock sacct array accounting for offline testing
Review checklist before retrying array tasks

skills.name: Slurm Array Retry Plan
skills.summary: Plan safe retries for failed Slurm array tasks.

skills.description: This skill helps users and support teams review CPU, memory, elapsed time, and requested resource signals for a completed Slurm job using read-only accounting commands.

examples.title: Collect a Slurm job efficiency report

skills.name: Slurm Efficiency Report
skills.summary: Summarize completed Slurm job efficiency from accounting data.

skills.description: This skill helps users and support teams diagnose Slurm GPU allocation, per-task GPU binding, CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES remapping, and vendor telemetry before scaling multi-GPU training, simulation, or MPI workloads.
skills.name: Slurm GPU Binding Diagnostics
skills.summary: Collect per-task GPU binding and visibility evidence from Slurm jobs.

skills.description: This skill helps users turn a manifest of independent inputs into a bounded Slurm job array with per-task logging, concurrency limits, and restart-friendly task selection.
skills.name: Slurm Job Array Patterns
skills.summary: Run parameter sweeps and many independent tasks with Slurm job arrays.

skills.description: This skill helps users plan and optionally submit small Slurm dependency chains for preprocessing, analysis, and postprocessing stages using afterok, afterany, and singleton patterns.

examples.title: Plan or submit a three-stage dependency chain

skills.name: Slurm Job Dependency Chain
skills.summary: Chain multi-stage Slurm jobs with explicit dependencies.

skills.description: This skill helps users and support teams distinguish planned maintenance, advanced reservations, drained nodes, down partitions, and unavailable resources from job-script or application failures using read-only Slurm commands.
skills.name: Slurm Maintenance Reservation Triage
skills.summary: Collect read-only Slurm evidence for maintenance windows and reservations.

skills.description: This skill provides read-only commands for checking queued, running, completed, and failed Slurm jobs using squeue, sacct, scontrol, and log files.
skills.name: Slurm Monitor Job
skills.summary: Inspect Slurm job state, accounting records, and output paths.

skills.description: This skill helps users and support teams diagnose Slurm NODE_FAIL, BOOT_FAIL, launch-failure, and node-related job failures by collecting accounting, job, node, partition, unavailable-node reason, and log evidence without changing scheduler or node state.
skills.name: Slurm Node Failure Triage
skills.summary: Collect read-only evidence for Slurm node-related job failures.

skills.description: This skill collects read-only Slurm accounting, job-state, optional efficiency, and log evidence for jobs that failed with out-of-memory or killed-process symptoms.

examples.title: Review memory request choices after triage

skills.name: Slurm OOM Memory Triage
skills.summary: Collect Slurm memory evidence for out-of-memory jobs.

skills.description: This skill helps users and support teams diagnose missing, empty, misplaced, or confusing Slurm stdout/stderr logs by collecting accounting, job-record, output-path, working-directory, and filesystem evidence without modifying job or user data.

examples.title: Review missing or empty output logs

skills.name: Slurm Output Log Triage
skills.summary: Collect read-only evidence for missing or confusing Slurm output logs.

skills.description: This skill helps users and support teams inspect pending Slurm jobs with squeue, scontrol, and optional sprio output so they can distinguish dependency, priority, resource, partition, reservation, and policy-related wait reasons.
skills.name: Slurm Pending Reason Triage
skills.summary: Explain why Slurm jobs are pending using read-only scheduler signals.

skills.description: This skill helps users make Slurm jobs signal-aware, checkpoint-friendly, and safe to restart when preemption, node failure, or an explicit requeue starts the batch script again with the same job id.
skills.name: Slurm Preemption And Requeue
skills.summary: Handle Slurm preemption signals and guarded requeue workflows.

skills.description: This skill helps users and support teams investigate pending jobs caused by Slurm association limits, QOS limits, invalid accounts, invalid QOS values, fairshare pressure, and TRES-minute policy without modifying scheduler state.

examples.title: Collect Slurm QOS and account limit evidence

skills.name: Slurm QOS Account Limit Triage
skills.summary: Collect read-only evidence for Slurm account, QOS, and fairshare limits.

skills.description: This skill uses sacct output to help users choose safer memory, CPU, and wall-time requests for similar Slurm jobs.
skills.name: Slurm Resource Estimator
skills.summary: Estimate future Slurm resource requests from accounting history.

skills.description: This skill helps users create Slurm sbatch scripts for CPU, GPU, MPI, and array workloads with conservative defaults and clear placeholders.
skills.name: Slurm Submit Job
skills.summary: Generate safe Slurm batch scripts for common HPC job shapes.

skills.description: This skill helps users and support teams diagnose Slurm TIMEOUT, walltime exhaustion, and near-time-limit jobs by collecting accounting, job-record, optional efficiency, and log evidence without modifying scheduler state.
skills.name: Slurm Time Limit Triage
skills.summary: Collect read-only evidence for Slurm time-limit failures.

skills.description: This skill provides a starter Snakemake Slurm profile and launch wrapper for cluster execution with conservative default resources.

skills.description: This skill provides a starter spack.yaml and commands for concretizing, installing, and loading a user-owned scientific software environment.

skills.summary: Create reproducible Spack environments for HPC software stacks.
